passkeyDialog.title: {{provider}} Authentication Required
passkeyDialog.description: You have a {{provider}} model configured. Please enter your 6-character passkey to decrypt and load the configuration.
passkeyDialog.lengthError: Passkey must be at least 6 characters
passkeyDialog.cancelConfirm:
Are you sure you want to cancel?

This will clear the selected {{provider}} model. You'll need to select a different model to continue.
passkeyDialog.helpText: This passkey was set when you first configured {{provider}}. If you've forgotten it, you'll need to reconfigure the provider.
passkeyDialog.masterDescription: Enter your master passkey to unlock all encrypted provider configurations ({{providers}}).
passkeyDialog.masterLengthHint: Passkey must be at least 6 characters ({{current}}/6)
passkeyDialog.masterCancelConfirm:
Are you sure you want to cancel?

You won't be able to use encrypted providers until you enter the master passkey.
passkeyDialog.masterHelpText: This passkey was set when you first configured an API provider. It protects all your encrypted configurations.

masterKeySetup.title: Create Master Passkey
masterKeySetup.description: Create a master passkey to protect all your API configurations. This passkey will be required on app startup.
masterKeySetup.infoText: Your master passkey encrypts all API keys with AES-256. Store it securely - it cannot be recovered if lost.
masterKeySetup.helpText: Choose a strong passkey you can remember. You'll need it each time you start the app.

errors.tooShort: Passkey must be at least 6 characters

description.setup: Create a single master passkey to protect all your API configurations.
description.migrate: Migrate your existing provider configurations to the new unified encryption system.
description.complete: Your configurations have been migrated successfully.

migration.masterKeyInfo: We're upgrading to a unified master key system. You'll only need to remember one passkey for all providers.
migration.enterOldPasskey: Enter the passkey you used when you originally configured {{provider}}.
migration.skipInfo: Skip this provider if you don't remember the passkey. You can reconfigure it later.

complete.title: Migration Complete!
complete.description: Your provider configurations are now protected by a single master passkey.
complete.skippedInfo: You'll need to reconfigure these providers with new API keys.

fsFeature.name: File System
fsFeature.description: Access files across /documents and /workspaces with full read/write/search capabilities.

documentsFsFeature.name: Documents File System
documentsFsFeature.description: Access and manage files in /documents with read, write, search, and folder operations.

documentsFeature.name: Documents (Legacy)
documentsFeature.description: [Legacy] Basic document tools. Use documents-fs-feature instead.

nodejsSandboxFeature.name: Node.js Sandbox
nodejsSandboxFeature.description: Execute JavaScript/TypeScript, run shell commands, install packages, and start servers in an isolated sandbox.

webFeature.name: Web Browser
webFeature.description: Browse the web, open URLs, read pages, interact with DOM, and search within pages using a browser session.

newsCollectionFeature.name: News Collection
newsCollectionFeature.description: Research and summarize news by searching the web and reading full articles from multiple sources.

travelPlannerFeature.name: Travel Planner
travelPlannerFeature.description: Research destinations and generate a day-by-day travel itinerary saved to /documents/travel/.

mealPlannerFeature.name: Meal Planner
mealPlannerFeature.description: Find real recipes and generate a weekly meal plan with a consolidated shopping list saved to /documents/meals/.

dailyBriefingFeature.name: Daily Briefing
dailyBriefingFeature.description: Generate a personalized morning briefing combining today's news with your personal knowledge graph.

jobApplicationFeature.name: Job Application Assistant
jobApplicationFeature.description: Read your resume and a job posting, then generate a tailored cover letter and resume suggestions.

plannerFeature.name: Planner
plannerFeature.description: Forces structured planning with item-by-item completion tracking. Agent must check all items before finishing.

multiAgentFeature.name: Multi-Agent Delegation
multiAgentFeature.description: Allow this agent to delegate focused subtasks to a selected set of existing child agents.

languageTutorFeature.name: Language Tutor
languageTutorFeature.description: Interactive language tutor that builds a learner profile, runs structured lessons with Q&A, scores answers, and tracks progress in the knowledge graph.

shoppingAssistantFeature.name: Shopping Assistant
shoppingAssistantFeature.description: Deep product research: searches multiple sources, reads prices, specs and reviews, compares alternatives, and saves a full report to /documents/shopping-assistant/.

financeTrackerFeature.name: Finance Tracker
financeTrackerFeature.description: Deep stock and company research with Mermaid diagrams and ASCII charts covering financials, valuation, analyst opinions, and news. Saves reports to /documents/finance/.

activeMemoryFeature.name: Active Memory
activeMemoryFeature.description: Remember, retrieve, update, remove, and explain durable memories in the currently selected topic graph.

hyperframesFeature.name: Video Creator
hyperframesFeature.description: Create animated browser-rendered video drafts with scene composition, preview playback, and MP4 export.

visualizeResponse.name: Visualize Response
visualizeResponse.description: Generate interactive OpenUI visualizations backed by knowledge graph data tools.

sandboxPanel.title: Live Workspace
sandboxPanel.description: Preview generated work, live pages, servers, and command activity.

copilot.error: useCopilot must be used within a CopilotProvider

welcome.title: Welcome to Memorall
welcome.content:
Memorall is a local-first AI workspace for chat, documents, agents, knowledge, and models.

This tour works before you choose a model, so you can understand the workspace first and pick your setup at the end.

chatPreview.title: Chat Preview
chatPreview.content:
Chat becomes the main conversation workspace after a model is ready.

This left side is where conversations, history, agent mode, attachments, and prompts come together.

layoutExplain.title: Split Workspace Layout
layoutExplain.content:
Memorall uses a split layout.

Left: chat and setup.
Center: the current conversation and input area.
Right: workspace pages like Documents, Knowledge Graph, Agents, Models, and Runtime.

documentsNavigate.title: Documents
documentsNavigate.content:
Next stop: Documents, your file library and document workspace.

Click Next to open it in the right panel.

documentsExplain.title: Document Library
documentsExplain.content:
Use Documents to upload, create, organize, and preview files.

Documents can become Knowledge Graph data, which gives agents useful memory for later conversations.

agentsNavigate.title: Agents
agentsNavigate.content:
Next: Agents, where you build reusable AI assistants for different workflows.

Click Next to open Agents.

agentsExplain.title: Custom Agents
agentsExplain.content:
Agents combine instructions, memory behavior, features, and tools into reusable workflows.

Use them when you want a consistent assistant for a repeated task or domain.

knowledgeNavigate.title: Knowledge Graph
knowledgeNavigate.content:
Next: Knowledge Graph, where Memorall shows stored memory as connected topics, entities, and facts.

Click Next to open it.

knowledgeExplain.title: Memory as a Graph
knowledgeExplain.content:
The Knowledge Graph helps you inspect what Memorall knows.

Search concepts, review relationships, filter by topic, and curate the connections agents can use later.

modelsNavigate.title: Models
modelsNavigate.content:
Next: Models, where you manage local models, browser models, local servers, and API providers.

Click Next to open Models.

modelsExplain.title: Model Setup
modelsExplain.content:
Models power chat and agent workflows.

You can run local browser models, connect local servers such as Ollama or LM Studio, or use remote API providers when you need stronger hosted models.

chatFinalNavigate.title: Back to First Setup
chatFinalNavigate.content:
Now let's return to the first screen.

The final steps explain the three setup options you choose from before chat becomes active.

chatSetup.title: Choose How to Start
chatSetup.content:
This first screen asks how you want to power Memorall.

You can use a managed service, run local models, or connect your own provider keys.

setupManaged.title: Managed Service
setupManaged.content:
Managed Service is the easiest path: sign in and let Memorall handle hosted model access and infrastructure.

Use this when you want the fastest setup with the least configuration.

setupLocal.title: Local Models
setupLocal.content:
Local Models keep work on your device and can run without API costs.

Use this when privacy, offline use, and local control are more important than hosted model power.

setupKeys.title: Your Own Keys
setupKeys.content:
Your Own Keys connects external providers such as OpenAI or OpenRouter.

Use this when you want cloud model quality while keeping control over provider choice, billing, and limits.
